q1.question: What is a memory on FamilyGarden?
q1.answer: A memory is a secure digital container that preserves your moments as texts, photos, videos and audio. It's your personal story, organized chronologically and ready to be passed on to future generations. Each memory is private and accessible only to people you authorize.

q2.question: Is my data secure?
q2.answer: Absolutely. Your data is encrypted with the highest standards (AES-256), backed up daily on secure servers, and you maintain full control over who can access it. Our commitment: your memories remain private.

q3.question: How does family sharing work?
q3.answer: You can create circles of trust (family, friends) and invite members by email. For each circle, you define permissions: read only, comments, or contribution. Members can add their own perspectives on your shared memories.

q4.question: What happens to my data if I don't renew?
q4.answer: Your data is never automatically deleted. If you switch from Premium to Free, you keep read access to all your content. Only advanced features are disabled. You can always export your data at any time.

q5.question: How does the posthumous legacy work?
q5.answer: With the Heritage offer, you designate trusted contacts who will be notified and receive access to certain memories according to your wishes. You can schedule delayed revelations, define access conditions, and leave personal messages.

q6.question: Is there a mobile app?
q6.answer: The web platform is fully responsive and works perfectly on mobile. A native iOS and Android app is planned in our roadmap to enable direct recording and offline mode.

beforeItems: Messages that fade away over time
Old hard drives, forgotten USB sticks
s1.afterTitle: You bring everything together, in one place
s1.afterDescription: Import photos, videos, audio and text in a few clicks. Family Garden becomes your family's single vault.
afterPoints: Drag and drop from phone or computer
Everything is private by default, hosted in Europe

s2.beforeTitle: Tomorrow: impossible to find a specific memory
beforeItems: "Granny's wedding — what year was it again?"
Photos mixed up across 15 years, no context
No one knows who's in the photo
s2.afterDescription: Automatic timeline, categories (childhood, wedding, travels…), family tree linked to memories. You find everything in seconds.
afterPoints: Timeline view by decade, year, month
Family tree with photos and birth places
People tags: Granny appears wherever she is

s3.beforeTitle: One day: everything will be lost
beforeItems: Stories that no one ever told
s3.afterDescription: Share private circles with your children, grandchildren, brothers and sisters. Schedule memories to be delivered later. Your memory continues.

Memories to be delivered on a specific date
Heritage mode: trusted guardians for what comes next

audience.title: A different answer for every
audience.subtitle: Family Garden adapts to your story — whether it's building a legacy, keeping track of a childhood or gathering a wedding.

families.title: Reconnecting generations around a shared story
families.problem: Grandparents' memories stay in their heads, parents' photos on their phones, and children know almost nothing about those who came before them.
families.promise: Family Garden brings grandparents, parents and children together in one private space: each adds their memories, and the whole family inherits them.

parents.title: Capture every "first time" before it fades
parents.problem: First steps, first words, evening giggles: it all goes so fast. Videos pile up in the camera roll, anecdotes get lost, and in ten years only fragments will remain.
parents.promise: Create your child's journal in seconds: photo, word, video, dated and told. A living book they can read one day.

legacy.title: Pass on what isn't written anywhere else
legacy.problem: Family stories, recipes, values, a parent's voice... All of it disappears with those who carry it, for lack of being recorded in time.
legacy.promise: Schedule the release of chosen memories, designate trusted loved ones, and leave a living legacy — not just a material one.

mariage.title: Relive the most beautiful day, beyond the photographer's album
mariage.problem: You receive hundreds of photos and videos from your guests, scattered across messaging apps, clouds and instant-share tools. Six months later, no one knows where they are.
mariage.promise: Centralize all the wedding media in a shared private album: guests, witnesses and family drop their memories in the same place, organized and saved.

voyage.title: Keep the adventures alive, not just the photos
voyage.problem: Coming back from a trip, you share a few photos on social media, then everything disappears into the camera roll. Anecdotes, encounters, laughter: forgotten.
voyage.promise: Tell every stage with photos, videos, audio and text. One day your children can reopen the logbook of your travels — as if they were there.

events.title: Keep the reunion alive long after the party ends
events.problem: Birthdays, christenings, summer reunions: everyone takes photos, no one really shares them. The magic of the moment evaporates in days.
events.promise: Open a space dedicated to the event, invite the family to drop their memories there, and keep it all in a private circle for life.

t3.quote: Everything is private, no ads, no algorithm. Finally a place where my memories truly belong to me.

testimonialsV3.title: Families who have rediscovered
testimonialsV3.subtitle: More than a thousand families use Family Garden to preserve and pass on their memories.

hero.name: Marie L.
hero.quote: For thirty years I kept whole boxes of photos, letters, audio cassettes. With Family Garden, I was finally able to gather everything in one place, tell each story in my own words, and share with my children and grandchildren. Today, my family knows its history — and I'm the one who passed it on to them.

thomas.name: Thomas D.
thomas.quote:
My children love watching memories together on Sunday evenings. It's become our family ritual. And everything is safe.
It matters to us.

sophie.name: Sophie M.
sophie.quote: I created an account for my grandmother. She's 82 and manages on her own thanks to the very simple interface. She sends us memories every week. A real gift.

jean.name: Jean P.
jean.quote: I was able to record my voice telling my childhood during the war. My great-grandchildren will be able to listen to me in fifty years. It's the most beautiful thing I'll leave behind.
